name: Duck with Chestnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Well beforehand, disjoint a large duck by cutting it into 2 breast pieces and 4 leg and thigh pieces. Use the back, neck, and wings to make a good strong stock. Remove the excess fat and render for use another day.
2. Parboil and peel 0.5 kg chestnuts.
3. Heat a large, dry frying pan to very hot and sauté the duck pieces until golden on all sides. Pour off all but 1 tablespoon of the accumulated fat, which will have rendered into the pan.
4. Gently fry a finely chopped large onion in the remaining fat until it is golden. Add the zest and juice of 2 large oranges and stir to deglaze the pan.
5. Put the duck legs into a large saucepan, pour the orange-onion mixture over them, and add enough duck stock to cover plus several sprigs of thyme. Simmer for 30 minutes.
6. Add the duck breasts and chestnuts, and if necessary, more stock to cover. Cook for 20 minutes longer or until fork-tender.
7. Remove the duck and chestnuts to a serving dish and keep warm while boiling the sauce hard to reduce until syrupy. Add a tablespoon of red currant jelly, adjust the seasoning, and pour the sauce over the duck and chestnuts. Serve.
